faqs.question: Can I use AI to summarize turning sales call transcripts into follow-up notes and next steps into a usable recap?
faqs.answer: Yes, but the recap should still be checked against the original notes. tl;dv is the best first pass for a structured recap, TextCortex is useful when you want a different summary angle, and DeepL Write helps turn rough notes into a cleaner client version.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better for action items?
faqs.answer: tl;dv is the better starting point when action items need to stay close to the source notes. Use TextCortex when you need a source-backed follow-up or a second look.

faqs.question: Is tl;dv better than TextCortex for client recaps?
faqs.answer: tl;dv is better when you already have notes from the meeting; TextCortex is better when the recap needs more context or a broader source check.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I send the recap?
faqs.answer: Do not send the recap until commitments, dates, owners, and next steps are confirmed. That is the fastest way to keep the summary useful.
